module_mood_contract: Operational clarity — organisers feel they own the synthesis path and know exactly how to run it

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity: organiser section is M4, separate from participant path.

rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[0].mood_score = 8. Operational clarity delivered. Prompt mechanics carry the mood without extra scaffolding.
trace.phases[1].mood_score = 8. Optional framing ('if time remains') correctly signals the bonus nature; mood holds.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M4 is operational clarity; it does not steal participant arc from M1/M2/M3.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Organiser-only section explicitly separated from participant path. No participant teaching beats present. Strategy doc: 'operational clarity... not a fourth participant module; it is the runbook.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands: synthesis-reading mechanic reliably triggers across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Phase 1 prompt handles the 'no files yet' case inline ('If no group files are ready, say so briefly, wait, and check shared/ again'), removing the main pre-flight hesitation. Persona executed without needing escape hatch.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity: 'same workbook carries the organiser runbook, but organiser work stays separate from the participant path.'
rules_evaluated.evidence: File is entirely organiser-scoped. No participant-path content present. Meta block explicitly: 'Audience: organisers only.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: this file is an optional epilogue after M3; no next module to hand off to.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4 is the final section of the workbook. No module N+1 exists in the claude-basics arc.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No authored conversation cues, comparison prompts, or 'discuss with...' sentences found. Body is entirely about the work (prompts to run, save instruction, nudge move).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 37: 'If the synthesis stops, nudge Claude with: check again' — the recovery forcing function (the nudge instruction) is in body prose between the fenced prompts, not inside a fenced block. A prompt-to-prompt reader will miss it and sit waiting indefinitely. Trace confirmed: persona confusion_flag on this line.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Move the nudge instruction inside the Phase 1 prompt fence (e.g. append 'If I send you "check again", re-read shared/ immediately and refresh the readout.') so Claude knows the protocol and the student instruction is prompt-adjacent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Heavy fan-out prompts get a body-side recovery callout.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 prompt includes continuous-poll behaviour ('After each update, wait briefly, check shared/ again'). Body already provides the lightweight recovery callout ('If the synthesis stops, nudge Claude with: check again'). The callout is present, even if its placement relative to the fence is sub-optimal (covered by rule 16 above).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 prompt: lead-in is 'Ask Claude to watch the group files and synthesize the latest read.' Phase 2 prompt 1: lead-in is 'Ask Claude for a few possible share-back angles.' Phase 2 prompt 2: lead-in is 'If the first answer gives you the right angle but not the words yet, ask Claude to draft the point in plain organiser language.' All three satisfy the rule.

Phase 2 prompt 2 (lines 64-74) ends with 'Let's focus on:' — the colon expects the organiser to type their chosen angle after pasting, which is a valid open-hook pattern. However the open-hook qualifier requires: 'Does the student have the value MORE concretely than Claude does?' — Yes, the organiser chose the angle from the prior exchange. PASS on the substantive open-hook test. BUT: the lead-in ('ask Claude to draft the point in plain organiser language') does NOT name the fill-in slot per the open-hook canonical shape ('Drop your chosen angle after the colon'). The second sentence of the lead-in must name the slot.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Append one clause to the lead-in: 'Drop your chosen angle after the colon in the prompt.' No change to the fenced block needed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All three fenced prompts use plain bullets and plain prose. No **bold** or *italic* inside the fences.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Hard-grep phrase list — no room-share commands.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No banned phrases detected in student-facing body. The file is organiser-only; no cohort-management language present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — optional moves marked optional.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 2 is explicitly framed as 'If time remains.' The 'Do not save until I say the readout is final' instruction preserves organiser control over the save step. Agency is respected throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Session widget shape: **Session** *(verb, "name")* at exercise top.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 7: **Session** *(new, "Organisers rollout synthesis")* — correct source shape, placed at exercise top before Phase 1.
